title: Catch the Sun!
subtitle: Solar Harvesting
description: The desert sun is the most powerful energy source in the UAE. Let's learn how Masdar City captures it!

questions.question: What do solar panels capture to make electricity?
questions.explanation: Solar panels capture sunlight and turn it into electricity!

questions.question: The UAE desert gets lots of sunshine, which makes it perfect for solar energy.
questions.explanation: True! The UAE has over 300 sunny days a year — perfect for solar power.

questions.question: Masdar City in Abu Dhabi is famous for being powered by what?
questions.explanation: Masdar City is the world's most sustainable city, powered by renewable energy!

questions.question: What color are most solar panels?
questions.explanation: Solar panels are dark blue or black because dark colors absorb more sunlight.

questions.question: Solar panels can only work when it is very hot outside.
questions.explanation: Solar panels need sunlight, not heat! They actually work better in cooler temperatures.

title: Ride the Wind!
subtitle: Wind Dynamics
description: The ancient Barjeel wind towers kept buildings cool for centuries in the UAE. Now we use wind for energy too!

questions.question: What is a 'Barjeel' in UAE architecture?
options: A wind tower that cools buildings
questions.explanation: A Barjeel is a traditional UAE wind tower that catches wind and cools buildings naturally!

questions.question: Wind turbines spin because of the force of moving air.
questions.explanation: Correct! Moving wind pushes the turbine blades, which spins a generator to create electricity.

questions.question: Which part of a wind turbine catches the wind?
questions.explanation: The long blades catch the wind and spin to generate electricity!

questions.question: Wind energy is an example of what type of energy?
questions.explanation: Wind is renewable — it never runs out and doesn't pollute the air!

questions.question: Traditional Barjeel wind towers and modern wind turbines both use wind to help people.
questions.explanation: Amazing! Both use wind — one for cooling, one for making electricity. Smart UAE thinking!

title: Save Every Drop!
subtitle: Water Desalination
description: The UAE is surrounded by saltwater seas but has very little fresh water. Desalination plants save the day!

questions.question: What does a desalination plant do?
questions.explanation: Desalination removes salt from seawater to make it safe to drink. Amazing science!

questions.question: The UAE gets most of its drinking water from rivers and lakes.
questions.explanation: The UAE has almost no rivers. Most drinking water comes from desalination plants!

questions.question: Can you drink water directly from the sea?
options: No, it has too much salt
questions.explanation: Seawater has too much salt for humans to drink — that's why we need desalination!

questions.question: At Masdar City, water is used very carefully. This is called?
questions.explanation: Water conservation means using water wisely and not wasting it. Every drop counts!

questions.question: It takes energy to turn saltwater into fresh water.
questions.explanation: Yes! That's why Masdar City uses solar power for its desalination — clean energy for clean water!

title: All Aboard the PRT!
subtitle: Public Transit
description: Masdar City has a Personal Rapid Transit system — small electric pods that move people without any pollution!

questions.question: What does PRT stand for in Masdar City?
questions.explanation: PRT stands for Personal Rapid Transit — small electric pods that take you anywhere in Masdar City!

questions.question: The PRT pods in Masdar City produce zero exhaust fumes.
questions.explanation: PRT pods run on electricity — no fuel, no fumes, no pollution. Clean travel!

questions.question: Why is public transport better for the environment than everyone driving alone?
questions.explanation: One bus or pod can carry many people, so fewer vehicles means less pollution in the air!

questions.question: Electric vehicles are better for the environment because they don't burn what?
questions.explanation: Electric vehicles don't burn petrol or diesel, so they don't release harmful gases!

questions.question: Cars and trucks that burn petrol contribute to air pollution.
questions.explanation: True! That's why Masdar City chose electric transport — cleaner air for everyone!

title: Plant the Future!
subtitle: Green Canopy
description: The Ghaf tree is the UAE's national tree. It grows in the desert and gives shade, oxygen, and life to all around it!

questions.question: What is the national tree of the UAE?
questions.explanation: The Ghaf tree is the UAE's national tree! It's incredibly tough and survives in the desert.

questions.question: Trees help clean the air by taking in carbon dioxide and releasing oxygen.
questions.explanation: Yes! Trees breathe in CO₂ and breathe out oxygen — they're nature's air purifiers!

questions.question: Planting trees in a city helps to reduce what problem?
questions.explanation: Trees provide shade and cool the air — perfect for fighting the UAE heat!

questions.question: What gas do plants and trees absorb from the air?
questions.explanation: Trees absorb carbon dioxide (CO₂) — a greenhouse gas that causes climate change. Trees are heroes!

questions.question: Masdar City has green spaces and parks to make life better for people and the environment.
questions.explanation: Absolutely! Green spaces in Masdar City cool the area, clean the air, and make people happy!
